title: 5 Managing Dishonoured PADs, Account Errors, and Chargebacks for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
5	Managing Dishonoured PADs, Account Errors, and Chargebacks for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
5.1	Managing Dishonoured Pre-Authorized Debits (PADs) for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
Central 1 creates PADs (the sweep) to debit Provincial Concentrator holding accounts and credit the Province of BCâs account at Central 1. If a Provincial Concentrator holding account PAD is dishonoured, contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialists immediately. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details. PADs must not be returned through the clearing.
5.2	Managing Deposit Errors for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Deposit Errors Identified by the Province of BC
If the Province of British Columbia identifies an error (e.g., a double posting, an incorrect amount, a wrong Location ID), it will request an adjustment from Central 1. Central 1 creates an AFT transaction and the adjustment appears on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report within two business days of processing the adjustment. Authorized users can access adjustment notices on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Deposit Errors Identified by your Financial Institution
If you discover an error (e.g., a PAD was processed for the wrong amount, or a duplicate Province of BC Deposit Slip was submitted), you must submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow. Central 1 will investigate the deposit and advise the Province of BC of the results. If Central 1 determines that an adjustment is required, an AFT transaction is initiated to the appropriate Provincial Concentrator holding account to balance the account. The adjustment appears on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report within two business days of processing the adjustment. Authorized users can access adjustment notices on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Note: 	You canât charge overdraft fees to the Provincial Concentrator holding account if an error caused a negative account balance. See Section 3.4, Reimbursement for Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Service Charges and Other Fees, for more information.

Your financial institution is responsible for clerical errors made during deposits to Provincial Concentrator holding accounts and when submitting a Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow. You are also responsible for monitoring a Provincial Concentrator holding account to ensure itâs at a zero balance each day and that all adjustments are processed. If an adjustment wasnât posted to the account, request an update on the open ServiceNow ticket or contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialist to determine if an adjustment is pending. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details.
5.3 	Managing Chargebacks for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
Cheques included in Provincial Concentrator holding account deposits must be returned to CIBC for processing if dishonoured. To ensure dishonoured items are returned to CIBC and not to your financial institution, you must enter the CIBC transit number (00090-010) in the return information fields when processing Provincial Concentrator holding account cheques in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
If you receive a misdirected chargeback for a Provincial Concentrator holding account, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow to request Central 1 to redirect the item to CIBC.
